article.description: — Devin Booker shook off a subpar shooting night, scoring 11 of his 27 points in the fourth quarter, and the Phoenix Suns overcame a 16-point deficit to beat the Washington Wizards 112-108 on Sunday night.
article.headline: Devin Booker sparks big 4th quarter, Suns rally to beat Wizards 112-108

images.caption: Jordan Poole brings the ball up but slips without anyone near him.
images.name: Oh no! Jordan Poole slips in open court

article.linkText: Devin Booker sparks big 4th quarter, Suns rally to beat Wizards 112-108
article.story: PHOENIX -- — Devin Booker shook off a subpar shooting night, scoring 11 of his 27 points in the fourth quarter, and the Phoenix Suns overcame a 16-point deficit to beat the Washington Wizards 112-108 on Sunday night. Booker missed six of seven 3-point attempts in the first three quarters, then connected on a pair as the Suns came back from an eight-point deficit to start the final period. “I get frustrated because I want to make every shot I take. But you just got to keep playing,” Booker said. "I've been doing this a long time. “That communication has to be there, and you want to take away the layups and force teams to make contested mid-range shots. There's firepower all around the league and once the team gets the early lead and gets confidence you never know what can happen.” Booker also had eight assists. “He had a tough first three quarters, but it was time for him to take over,” Suns coach Frank Vogel said. “It’s when he is at his best.” Kevin Durant led the Suns with 28 points, including a dunk on a three-point play in the final minute. Jusuf Nurkic had 17 rebounds. Drew Eubanks scored 12 points while Jordan Goodwin grabbed eight rebounds and had six assists off the bench. The Suns have blown several fourth quarter leads this season but came back this time to outscore Washington 31-19 in the fourth to snap a two-game losing streak and stay above .500 at 14-12. “Tonight was a step in the right direction,” Durant said. “We want to build off of it and continue getting better.” The Suns were 7 of 31 from 3-point range and committed 17 turnovers. “That could be deflating to anybody,” Durant said of the first-half struggles, which saw Phoenix trail by 16 at one point. “We missed 3s in the first half but I felt like all of them were open shots,” he said. “We made more shots in the second half and that just made everything look better.” Daniel Gafford had 26 points and 17 rebounds for the Wizards, who dropped to 4-21 in a failed bid to win consecutive games for the first time this season. Washington beat Indiana on Friday night after losing 15 of its previous 16 games. “He was unbelievable," Wizards coach Wes Unseld Jr. said about Gafford. "He takes a beating some nights and he just keeps going. Always quarterbacking the defense and protecting the rim. He made play after play to keep us in it.” Tyus Jones had 22 points for Washington, and Kyle Kuzma added 17 before fouling out. Former Wizard Bradley Beal sat out for the Suns after spraining his right ankle Friday night against New York. He had just returned in the Suns’ previous game after missing 12 games because of a back injury. Suns coach Frank Vogel said before the game there should be an update Monday on Beal’s status. With Beal out, Nassir Little made his first start of the season. Eric Gordon returned to score 13 points after missing the last two games because of a right leg contusion. NEXT Wizards: At Sacramento on Monday night. Phoenix: At Portland on Tuesday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

articles.description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks as the favorites to win the NBA championship.
articles.headline: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Spurs favored vs. Knicks entering Finals

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder are back to being odds-on title favorites after taking a 3-2 series lead over the Spurs.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [608x342]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: New York Knicks guard Landry Shamet (L) defends Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (R) during game 3 of the NBA East Conference Finals between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the New York Knicks in Cleveland, Ohio, USA, 23 May 2026.
images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.
images.name: Knicks/Cavs [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) takes a shot as San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) defends during the first quarter during game two of the western conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Paycom Center.
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander how has even odds to win the Finals MVP.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander  [1296x729]

images.alt: VIctor Wembanyama
images.caption: VIctor Wembanyama poured in 41 points and grabbed 24 boards in the Spurs' thrilling Game 1 win.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Williams [608x342] - Copy

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) looks for an outlet against San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) during the first half of an NBA basketball game, Tuesday, Jan. 13, 2026, in Oklahoma City.
images.caption: The Thunder enter the Western Conference Finals as the odds-on favorite to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
